title: Token-Level Entropies
concept: Monitoring token probability distributions over time detects when the model drifts into nonsense or collapses to a single repeating word.

exercises.prompt: What does 'token-level entropy' measure in an AI text model?

options.text: The uncertainty or spread of the next-word options
The speed at which the AI types words on the screen
The number of spelling mistakes in the paragraph

exercises.explain: Entropy measures the uncertainty or variety in the AI's upcoming word choices. Measuring it at each token (word) step is called token-level entropy.

cards.title: When AIs Go Loop-de-Loop
cards.body: Have you ever seen an AI chat assistant suddenly lose its mind and start repeating the same word over and over again? We can actually predict and detect this weird behavior before it happens by measuring the 'chaos' or choices in its brain!

cards.title: What is Token Entropy?
cards.body: Token-level entropy measures how uncertain an AI is when choosing the next word. A high entropy means the AI sees many likely choices. A low entropy means the AI is extremely confident in just one or two words. Monitoring this over time helps us spot when the AI is losing its mind or getting stuck.

cards.title: The One-Spice Chef
cards.body: Imagine a chef cooking. If they have a balanced spice rack, they choose from many ingredients (high entropy). If they suddenly throw away all spices and only use salt for every single dish, their cooking style has collapsed (low entropy). Similarly, when an AI collapses to repeating the same word, its options shrink down to just one choice.

cards.title: Catching the Loop
cards.body: If an AI is writing a creative story, you want a healthy, medium-to-high entropy because many different words could make sense next. But if you monitor the logs and see the entropy suddenly drop to absolute zero, you'll likely open the output and find: 'the the the the the...'

cards.title: Is Low Entropy Always Good?
cards.body: Many think that low entropy is always a good thing because it means the AI is confident. But if the entropy stays too low for too long, it usually means the AI has frozen up and is repeating itself, creating boring or broken text.

cards.title: Probability Distributions
cards.body: Let's compare the word choices of an AI under healthy conditions versus during a repetitive collapse.

left.text: The AI has multiple words with similar probabilities (e.g., 'dog': 30%, 'cat': 25%, 'bird': 20%).

right.text: One word has a 99.9% probability and everything else is 0% (e.g., 'the': 99.9%).

exercises.prompt: What does it mean when an AI has high token-level entropy?

options.text: The AI is uncertain and has many different words it could choose next.
The AI is absolutely certain of only one single word.
The AI has shut down and is not generating any text.
The AI is running out of computer memory.

exercises.explain: High token entropy means the probability distribution is spread out over many potential words, indicating uncertainty or variety.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Monitoring token entropy over time helps us detect when a model drifts into nonsense or repetitive loops.
exercises.explain: By tracking entropy over time, we can flag sudden drops (repetition) or sudden spikes (nonsense) to stop the AI before the user sees the bad output.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the explanation of AI monitoring:
exercises.sentence: To catch an AI getting stuck in a repetitive loop, we can monitor its token-level [blank].
exercises.explain: Token-level entropy measures the randomness or uncertainty in the AI's word choices. A sudden drop signals a repetitive loop.

exercises.prompt: Which of these AI outputs shows a clear sign of collapsed token-level entropy?

options.text: "Once upon a time, there was a brave little seedling."
"with with with with with with with with with"
"The temperature tomorrow will be twenty degrees."

exercises.explain: The sentence 'the the the the' shows zero variety and total repetition, which is a classic symptom of collapsed entropy.

exercises.prompt: If an AI model enters a repetitive loop and outputs the word "the" over and over again, what happens to its token-level entropy?

options.text: The entropy spikes to its maximum possible value.
The entropy drops down to zero.
The entropy fluctuates wildly up and down.
The entropy stays exactly at a healthy average.

exercises.explain: When an AI gets stuck repeating the exact same word, its confidence for that specific word becomes 100%, and the probability for all other words drops to 0%. This complete lack of variety causes the token entropy to collapse to zero.
